# The line below allows to print all the outputs of a cell instead of only the last one
# %config InteractiveShell.ast_node_interactivity = "all"
import os
import pathlib
import sys

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import yaml
In [2]:
from_script = True

print(f"WARNING: This notebook is set with from_script = {from_script}.")

if from_script:
    # Get the path of the notebook config file from the environment variable
    path_config_notebook = os.environ["PATH_YAML_CONFIG"]
    # Load the notebook config file
    with open(path_config_notebook, "r") as file:
        dict_config_notebook = yaml.safe_load(file)
        print("The dict_config_notebook is:")
        print(dict_config_notebook)
    ID_XP_DATASET = dict_config_notebook["id_xp_dataset"]
    ID_XP = dict_config_notebook["id_xp"]
    PATH_PROJECT = pathlib.Path(dict_config_notebook["path_project"])
    DICT_FILTERS = dict_config_notebook.get("dict_filters", None)
    NAME_MLFLOW_STORE = dict_config_notebook.get(
        "name_mlflow_store", "mlruns_ruche_downloaded"
    )
    RELATIVE_PATH_INFERENCE_FOLDER_RUN_ID = dict_config_notebook["relative_path_inference_folder_run_id"]

    DEBUG_SIZE_FILTER = dict_config_notebook.get("debug_size_filter", None)
else:
    ID_XP_DATASET = 21
    ID_XP = 24
    RELATIVE_PATH_INFERENCE_FOLDER_RUN_ID = (
        "data/mlruns/1/23410049a392403d849b0aaf156d66e2/generated_data/run_id"
    )
    PATH_PROJECT = pathlib.Path(
        "/home/hosseinkhan/Documents/work/phd/git_repositories/control_dde"
    )
    # DICT_FILTERS = {
    #     "env_name": ["cylinder"],
    #     "env_params_max_control": [0.0],
    #     "env_params_reynolds": [120.0, 90.0],
    # }
    DICT_FILTERS = {}
    DEBUG_SIZE_FILTER = None
    NAME_MLFLOW_STORE = "mlruns_ruche_downloaded"

assert (
    DEBUG_SIZE_FILTER is None or DEBUG_SIZE_FILTER > 0
), "DEBUG_SIZE_FILTER must be positive."


# Add the path of the project to the sys.path in order to import
# the modules in the src folder
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath(PATH_PROJECT))

# Define the paths
path_mlruns = pathlib.Path(f"{PATH_PROJECT}/data/{NAME_MLFLOW_STORE}")
path_experiment_dataset = pathlib.Path(f"{path_mlruns}/{ID_XP_DATASET}")
path_experiment = pathlib.Path(f"{path_mlruns}/{ID_XP}")
# Get all folder in the mlruns/id_xp directory except the folder 'tags'
# using pathlib and iter dir
list_id_hash = [
    path.name
    for path in path_experiment.iterdir()
    if path.is_dir() and path.name != "tags"
]
list_id_hash_dataset = [
    path.name
    for path in path_experiment_dataset.iterdir()
    if path.is_dir() and path.name != "tags"
]

print(f"Number of runs in the dataset: {len(list_id_hash_dataset)} \n")
print(f"Number of runs: {len(list_id_hash)} \n")
print(f"XP id dataset: {ID_XP_DATASET} \n")
print(f"XP id: {ID_XP} \n")

nested_dict_config_dataset = {}
nested_dict_config = {}
list_df_config_flattened_dataset = []
list_df_config_flattened = []


def check_xp_lifecycle(path_file: pathlib.Path):
    if not path_file.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"The file {path_file} does not exist.")
    if not path_file.is_file():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"The path {path_file} is not a file.")
    # Load the yaml file
    with open(path_file, "r") as _file:
        dict_temp = yaml.safe_load(_file)
    # Check the lifecycle key
    assert dict_temp.get("lifecycle_stage") == "active", "The experiment is not active."


# Verify all experiments are done on the same environment:
path_xp = f"{path_mlruns}/{ID_XP}"
for name_id in list_id_hash:
    # Check file lifecycle
    list_glob_xp_metadata = list(pathlib.Path(f"{path_xp}/{name_id}").glob("meta.yaml"))
    assert len(list_glob_xp_metadata) != 0, "No metadata file in the directory."
    assert (
        len(list_glob_xp_metadata) == 1
    ), "More than one metadata file in the directory."
    check_xp_lifecycle(list_glob_xp_metadata[0])

    # Get the config file
    list_glob_config = list(
        pathlib.Path(f"{path_xp}/{name_id}/artifacts").glob("./config/*.yaml")
    )
    assert len(list_glob_config) != 0, "No config file in the directory."
    assert len(list_glob_config) == 1, "More than one config file in the directory."

    name_config_file = list_glob_config[0].name
    # Check if the file is a yaml file
    assert name_config_file.endswith(".yaml"), "File is not a yaml file."

    path_run_config_yaml = list_glob_config[0]
    with open(path_run_config_yaml, "r") as file:
        # Get the config file as a dictionary
        dict_config_temp = yaml.safe_load(file)
        # Add the config file to the nested dictionary
        nested_dict_config[name_id] = dict_config_temp
        # Flatten the config file for easier check
        list_df_config_flattened.append(pd.json_normalize(dict_config_temp, sep="_"))
    del dict_config_temp

path_xp_dataset = f"{path_mlruns}/{ID_XP_DATASET}"
for name_id in list_id_hash_dataset:
    list_glob_config = list(
        pathlib.Path(f"{path_xp_dataset}/{name_id}/generated_data").glob(
            "./config/*.yaml"
        )
    )
    assert len(list_glob_config) != 0, "No config file in the directory."
    assert len(list_glob_config) == 1, "More than one config file in the directory."

    name_config_file = list_glob_config[0].name
    # Check if the file is a yaml file
    assert name_config_file.endswith(".yaml"), "File is not a yaml file."

    path_run_config_yaml = list_glob_config[0]
    with open(path_run_config_yaml, "r") as file:
        # Get the config file as a dictionary
        dict_config_temp = yaml.safe_load(file)
        # Add the config file to the nested dictionary
        nested_dict_config_dataset[name_id] = dict_config_temp
        # Flatten the config file for easier check
        list_df_config_flattened_dataset.append(
            pd.json_normalize(dict_config_temp, sep="_")
        )
    del dict_config_temp

DataFrame: Training config¶

In [3]:
df_trainining_config = (
    pd.concat(
        [
            df_config_flattened.assign(training_id=name_id)
            for name_id, df_config_flattened in zip(
                list_id_hash, list_df_config_flattened
            )
        ]
    )
    .set_index("training_id")
    .pipe(
        lambda df: df.astype(
            {col: "float" for col in df.select_dtypes(include="int").columns}
        )
    )
)

DataFrame: Training and dataset config merged¶

Filtering DataFrame¶

In [6]:
# Filtering function based on the DICT_FILTERS
def filter_dataframe(df: pd.DataFrame):
    _mask = pd.Series([True] * len(df), index=df.index)
    if DICT_FILTERS is None:
        return _mask
    else:
        for key, list_values in DICT_FILTERS.items():
            if key in df.columns:
                _mask = _mask & df[key].isin(list_values)
            else:
                print(f"Warning: The key {key} is not in the config file.")
    return _mask


df_training_dataset_merged = (
    df_trainining_config.assign(
        dataset_id=lambda _x: _x.data_path_data_folder.str.extract(
            rf"{ID_XP_DATASET}/(.*?)/generated_data"
        )
    )
    .join(df_dataset_config, on="dataset_id", rsuffix="_dataset")
    .fillna({"model_params_n_delays": 0.0})
    # .loc[lambda df: filter_dataframe(df)]
)

Duplicates check and patch¶

In [9]:
# Get the number of duplicates
nb_duplicates = df_training_dataset_merged.duplicated().sum()
print(f"Number of duplicates: {nb_duplicates}")

# Filter the duplicates and keep the first occurence
df_training_dataset_merged = df_training_dataset_merged.loc[
    ~df_training_dataset_merged.duplicated()
]

Time series visualisation¶

In [11]:
# Loop over the subDataFrame determined byt the environment
path_run_id_inference = f"{PATH_PROJECT}/{RELATIVE_PATH_INFERENCE_FOLDER_RUN_ID}"

N_TRAJECTORIES_PLOT_START = 2
N_TRAJECTORIES_PLOT_END = 3
# STR_NORMALISATION_MODE = "unnormalised_data"
STR_NORMALISATION_MODE = "normalised_data"

for name_env, df_env in df_training_dataset_merged.groupby("env_name"):
    print(f"Environment: {name_env}")
    print(df_env.shape)
    # Get the non constant columns
    list_non_constant_columns = [
        col for col in df_env.columns if df_env[col].nunique() > 1
    ]
    # Remove the model_name column and model_params_n_delays
    list_non_constant_columns.remove("model_name")
    list_non_constant_columns.remove("trainer_name")
    list_non_constant_columns.remove("dataset_id")
    list_non_constant_columns.remove("data_path_data_folder")
    list_non_constant_columns.remove(
        "model_params_n_delays"
    )  # TODO: this could be attached to the model name...

    # Now extract each combination of the non constant columns
    dict_unique_values_by_column = {
        col: df_env[col].unique() for col in list_non_constant_columns
    }
    # Get the product of the unique values
    import itertools

    list_combinations = list(itertools.product(*dict_unique_values_by_column.values()))
    print(f"Number of combinations: {len(list_combinations)}")
    # Create a mask for each combination
    for combination in list_combinations:
        mask = pd.Series([True] * len(df_env), index=df_env.index)
        for col, value in zip(dict_unique_values_by_column.keys(), combination):
            # Print unique values for each column only if there are more than one unique value
            if len(dict_unique_values_by_column[col]) > 1:
                print(f"{col}: {value}")
            mask = mask & (df_env[col] == value)
        df_temp = df_env.loc[mask]
        print(f"Number of rows for the combination {combination}: {df_temp.shape[0]}")
        # Now get the data for the time series for each model

        dict_obs_trajectory_temp = {}
        dict_control_trajectory_temp = {}
        dict_time_trajectory_temp = {}

        # Now get the data for the time series for each model
        path_trajectory_temp = None
        array3d_obs = None
        array3d_control = None
        array_time = None
        n_points = None
        for name_model, df_model in df_temp.groupby("model_name"):
            # Get the data for the time series
            # # Get the path of the trajectory
            name_run_id = df_model.index[0]
            path_trajectory_temp = f"{path_run_id_inference}/{name_run_id}"
            print("Path trajectory temp:", path_trajectory_temp)

            # Load the trajectory
            array3d_obs = np.load(
                f"{path_trajectory_temp}/{STR_NORMALISATION_MODE}/predictions.npy"
            )
            n_points = array3d_obs.shape[1]
            array_time = np.load(
                f"{path_trajectory_temp}/{STR_NORMALISATION_MODE}/time.npy"
            )[0, -(n_points + 1) : -1]
            array3d_control = np.load(
                f"{path_trajectory_temp}/{STR_NORMALISATION_MODE}/controls.npy"
            )[:, -n_points:, :]
            dict_obs_trajectory_temp[name_model] = array3d_obs
            dict_control_trajectory_temp[name_model] = array3d_control
            dict_time_trajectory_temp[name_model] = array_time

        assert path_trajectory_temp is not None, "The path_trajectory_temp is None."
        assert array3d_obs is not None, "The array3d_obs is None."
        assert array3d_control is not None, "The array3d_control is None."
        assert array_time is not None, "The array_time is None."
        assert n_points is not None, "The n_points is None."

        dict_obs_trajectory_temp["truth"] = np.load(
            f"{path_trajectory_temp}/{STR_NORMALISATION_MODE}/observations.npy"
        )[:, -(n_points + 1) : -1, :]
        dict_time_trajectory_temp["truth"] = np.load(
            f"{path_trajectory_temp}/{STR_NORMALISATION_MODE}/time.npy"
        )[0, -(n_points + 1) : -1]
        dict_control_trajectory_temp["truth"] = np.load(
            f"{path_trajectory_temp}/{STR_NORMALISATION_MODE}/controls.npy"
        )[:, -n_points:, :]

        # Assert that the shapes are the same
        assert all(
            array3d_obs.shape == dict_obs_trajectory_temp[name_model].shape
            for name_model in dict_obs_trajectory_temp.keys()
        )
        assert all(
            array3d_control.shape == dict_control_trajectory_temp[name_model].shape
            for name_model in dict_control_trajectory_temp.keys()
        )
        assert all(
            array_time.shape == dict_time_trajectory_temp[name_model].shape
            for name_model in dict_time_trajectory_temp.keys()
        )

        dim_obs = array3d_obs.shape[-1]
        dim_control = array3d_control.shape[-1]
        # Plot the time series
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(dim_obs + dim_control, 1, figsize=(16, 12))
        for dim in range(dim_obs):
            for name_model in list(dict_obs_trajectory_temp.keys()):
                for i in range(N_TRAJECTORIES_PLOT_START, N_TRAJECTORIES_PLOT_END):
                    ax[dim].plot(
                        dict_time_trajectory_temp[name_model],
                        dict_obs_trajectory_temp[name_model][i, :, dim],
                        label=DICT_VALUE_DISPLAY_NAME[name_model],
                        color=COLOR_PALETTE[name_model],
                    )
            ax[dim].set_xlabel(rf"$\continuousTimePoint$")
            ax[dim].set_ylabel(rf"$\observationProcess^{dim}_\continuousTimePoint$")
            ax[dim].legend()
        for dim in range(dim_control):
            for name_model in list(dict_control_trajectory_temp.keys()):
                for i in range(N_TRAJECTORIES_PLOT_START, N_TRAJECTORIES_PLOT_END):
                    ax[dim + dim_obs].plot(
                        dict_time_trajectory_temp[name_model],
                        dict_control_trajectory_temp[name_model][i, :, dim],
                        label=DICT_VALUE_DISPLAY_NAME[name_model],
                        color=COLOR_PALETTE[name_model],
                    )
            ax[dim + dim_obs].set_xlabel(rf"$\continuousTimePoint$")
            ax[dim + dim_obs].set_ylabel(
                rf"$\controlProcess^{dim}_\continuousTimePoint$"
            )

        plt.show()
